steps.title: Track accuracy versus charm
steps.content: The passage compares historians' knowledge to a map: accurate and useful, but “cold,” “naked,” and lacking the charm of a painting. It then says historians are barred from methods that might repair those defects because they must preserve biographical exactness.

steps.title: Reject off-topic readings
steps.content: A treats the issue as a lack of skill, not a constraint created by truthfulness. B discusses fame, which never appears. C takes the map image literally instead of as an analogy. The central idea is that fidelity to truth can make historical writing less engaging. The answer is D.
